text: Fill in the blanks about respiratory physiology.
questions: The total lung capacity is the sum of *vital capacity* and *residual volume*.
Oxygen is transported in blood mainly bound to *haemoglobin* (98.5%) and a small amount dissolved in *plasma* (1.5%).
The oxygen-haemoglobin dissociation curve shifts to the right (decreased affinity) with increased *temperature*, increased CO2, decreased *pH*, and increased 2,3-DPG.
Ventilation is controlled primarily by the *medullary* respiratory centre, with peripheral chemoreceptors in the *carotid* body responding to low PaO2.
Carbon dioxide is transported in blood as *bicarbonate* (70%), carbaminohaemoglobin (23%), and dissolved in plasma (7%).

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
